metrics_glossary.resolved_count_per_category_max: max resolved across any single heuristic category
metrics_glossary.resolved_count_per_wallet_max: max resolved across any qualified smart-money wallet

items.title: Trade Reconciliation
items.action: Reconcile paper signals against actual Kalshi fills to confirm each signal has a corresponding real trade.
items.notes: Must be completed before position-reconciliation-job can safely automate the same check.

items.title: Real Fill Deduplication
items.action: Audit real_fill rows in leviathan.db and remove duplicate fills that do not match actual positions held.
items.notes: Duplicate fills skew PnL tracking and inflate fill counts used by downstream gates.

items.title: Sample Size Gates
items.action: Document the minimum resolved-signal thresholds that gate each downstream analysis step.
items.notes: Prevents drawing statistical conclusions before sample sizes support reliable estimates.

items.title: Wilson Confidence Intervals
items.action: Add Wilson score confidence intervals to win-rate stats in the email report.
items.notes: Point estimates are misleading at small sample sizes and Wilson intervals are well-calibrated.

items.title: Title Scraping Fix
items.action: Fix market title capture so titles are populated correctly for all logged signal rows.
items.notes: Blank titles break Power BI filtering and make signals unidentifiable in the dashboard.

items.title: Smart Money Drift Alerts
items.action: Alert when a tracked wallet materially shifts position size or direction between daily scans.
items.notes: Direction reversals by high-volume wallets are the strongest signal of new market information.

items.title: Backtest Harness
items.action: Build a framework to replay historical signals against resolved market outcomes.
items.notes: Required foundation before walk-forward-validation can be attempted.

items.title: Position Reconciliation Job
items.action: Automate daily reconciliation of open paper signals against the Kalshi position API.
items.notes: Depends on trade-reconciliation being clean first so automation does not propagate existing errors.

items.title: Brier Score Tracking
items.action: Track Brier scores over time to measure forecast calibration quality across runs.
items.notes: 25 resolved signals gives a stable enough baseline to detect systematic miscalibration.

items.title: Confluence Detection
items.action: Flag and track signals where multiple independent sources agree on direction.
items.notes: Multi-source agreement historically lifts win rates and needs resolved data to measure the effect.

items.title: Edge Decay Analysis
items.action: Measure how quickly identified edges decay as markets approach their resolution date.
items.notes: 30 resolved signals needed to fit a decay curve with acceptable variance.

items.title: Per-Wallet Track Record
items.action: Build per-wallet win rate and PnL tables using resolved signals aligned to wallet positions.
items.notes: 10 resolved signals per wallet is the minimum for a meaningful individual track record.

items.title: Skill vs Luck Weighting
items.action: Apply a binomial skill test to wallet track records to separate genuine skill from luck.
items.notes: Requires the same per-wallet sample size as per-wallet-track-record before the test has power.

items.title: Walk-Forward Validation
items.action: Run rolling out-of-sample validation on the scoring model using the backtest harness.
items.notes: Requires backtest-harness to exist before rolling windows can be executed.

items.title: Slippage Tracking
items.action: Track the difference between signal entry price and actual fill price across all real fills.
items.notes: 20 fills gives a stable estimate of average slippage for bid-ask spread modeling.

items.title: Calibration Curve
items.action: Plot predicted probabilities against actual win rates in decile buckets.
items.notes: 50 resolved signals needed to populate all decile buckets with meaningful counts.

items.title: Calibration Curve Dashboard
items.action: Add the calibration curve chart to the Power BI dashboard.
items.notes: Depends on calibration-curve analysis being complete before the visual can be built.

items.title: Per-Heuristic Scorecard
items.action: Build a per-heuristic win rate and PnL scorecard broken down by flag_path category.
items.notes: 15 resolved signals per category is the minimum for reliable category-level comparisons.

items.title: Heuristic Sunsetting
items.action: Disable or reweight heuristics that consistently underperform relative to base rates.
items.notes: Requires per-category sample size before sunsetting decisions are statistically reliable.

items.title: Empirical Base Rates from Polymarket
items.action: Replace heuristic base rates with empirical rates derived from Polymarket historical outcomes.
items.notes: Empirical rates reduce dependence on subjective prior calibration and should improve edge estimates.

items.title: Auto-Calibration Loop
items.action: Automatically adjust heuristic confidence weights based on tracked Brier scores and category win rates.
items.notes: Requires both sufficient global and per-category sample sizes before automation is safe.

items.title: Smart money wallet tracking page
items.action: Add a Power BI page showing realized hit rate over time per qualified smart-money wallet
items.notes: Viz layer on top of the per-wallet metric, gated so it only surfaces once wallets have enough resolved cross-refs to be readable

items.title: High-price filter (market_price >= 0.85)
items.action: Filter out markets at or above 0.85 market price before writing to DB

items.title: EV per contract display in signals and top picks
items.action: Show EV/contract in signal blocks and top picks summary

items.title: Betting queue block in daily report
items.action: Show top 5 unplaced signals sorted by urgency in daily report

items.title: Gate Unlock Notifier
items.action: Email once when a BACKLOG.md gate transitions locked/unknown -> unlocked, reusing the existing report email path.
items.notes: Deterministic notifier only -- forms no opinions, changes no thresholds. resolved_count_per_wallet_max stays not-measurable until per-wallet-track-record ships.

items.title: Show Detail Fix
items.action: Decouple show_detail in compile_report from the scanner qualifying count; gate it on whether smart-money data itself has signals, so trader detail stops silently vanishing during signal dry spells.
items.notes: core/report.py:1159 currently passes show_detail=len(qualifying) > 0 to _smart_money_section -- ties smart-money detail visibility to an unrelated scanner metric.

items.title: Discovery Funnel Diagnostic
items.action: Per-stage drop-off counter + gating-metric distributions for discover_winners; diagnoses why the winner gate finds zero (sample sourcing vs. skill genuinely rare). No threshold/gate changes. Unblocks the locked per-wallet items.
items.notes: Shipped as sources.accounts.diagnose_discovery() / format_diagnostic_report() + scripts/diagnose_discovery.py. Real run found mass attrition is sample-sourcing-driven (median resolved_count=0 across scored wallets) compounded by a long-shot-betting subpopulation among the few that clear the resolved_count gate -- see docs/PROGRESS.md.

items.title: Kalshi Event Ticker Capture
items.action: Persist event_ticker (already fetched at scan time) onto every logged signal; investigate the real kalshi.com market-page URL pattern.
items.notes: UPDATE 2026-07-23: URL pattern now confirmed -- https://kalshi.com/markets/{series_ticker}/{event_ticker}, backed by Kalshi's own AGENTS.md, its sitemap-markets.xml, and a genuine server-side redirect from /events/{event_ticker} that resolves real tickers but not fake ones. series_ticker (event-object-only field) now captured and threaded through end to end; kalshi_market_url() returns real links. See docs/PROGRESS.md 2026-07-23 (Kalshi Market-Link Pattern Confirmed) for the full evidence trail; 2026-07-22 entry has the original (since-superseded) investigation.

items.title: Email HTML Render
items.action: Render the daily report as email-safe HTML (multipart/alternative) matching the signed-off design, consuming goal_1 Kalshi links, sharing computed values with the text renderer so the two bodies can never diverge.
items.notes: No number changed -- render_html and compile_report both call shared _rank_top_picks/_betting_queue_data/_header_data. Kalshi links inert until kalshi_market_url resolves a real pattern (see kalshi-event-ticker-capture). See docs/PROGRESS.md 2026-07-23 for full report + human testing checklist.

items.title: Market-Price Baseline Brier
items.action: For every resolved signal, compute and persist the Brier score of the market price at scan time alongside the existing scorer Brier. Expose our_estimate, brier_scorer and brier_market as explicit columns in data/powerbi_export/signals.csv, and surface both Brier figures in analysis/calibration.py output.
items.notes: The scorer prompt injects current market price (core/scorer.py:649) and explicitly instructs anchoring to it (scorer.py:245-253), so scorer Brier alone cannot distinguish real edge from price echo. Arithmetic on data already in the DB, so no gate. Must exist before resolved_count clears the brier-tracking gate at 25. SHIPPED 2026-07-23: market_baseline_brier column (core/logger.py, additive migration), computed+persisted at resolution time in resolve_outcomes() and via backfill_market_baseline_brier() for the 10 pre-existing resolved rows with a usable market_price (1 of 11 has no market_price and is correctly left NULL, never coerced to 0.5). get_market_baseline_brier_score() mirrors get_brier_score() exactly. Surfaced in analysis/calibration.py (side by side with scorer Brier, plus a verdict line) and in the signals.csv export (scorer_brier computed column + persisted market_baseline_brier). Real result on the current n=8 paper population: scorer Brier 0.0578 vs baseline 0.0022 -- scorer currently WORSE than the market price, exactly the anchoring risk this metric exists to catch. See docs/PROGRESS.md 2026-07-23.

items.title: Pre-Registered Kill Criteria
items.action: Write docs/PREREGISTRATION.md stating, in advance and dated, the result at n=50 that would falsify the edge hypothesis and halt signal development. Specify the metric (scorer Brier vs market-baseline Brier), the margin required, and what happens if it is not met.
items.notes: No code. Worthless if written after the data is seen. Same discipline as the IDEAS.md append-only rule, applied to results instead of scope. Append-only once committed.

items.title: LLM Daily Cost Ceiling
items.action: Add a configurable daily spend cap in core/llm.py that accumulates cost_usd across calls and raises once breached. Surface the running total in the daily report footer.
items.notes: Per-call cost is computed at core/llm.py:146 but nothing enforces a limit. Prerequisite for replay-runner, which will multiply scoring volume.

items.title: Settled Market Fetcher
items.action: Pull Kalshi settled markets with their final outcomes, reaching further back than the local snapshot archive begins. Persist to a dedicated table separate from live signals.
items.notes: Read-only against existing tables. Local snapshots only reach 2026-07-08, so the archive alone caps corpus size well below what is useful.

items.title: As-Of Market State Reconstruction
items.action: Given a ticker and a historical date, reconstruct the market state as it stood then, sourcing from data/snapshots where available and Kalshi history beyond that. Output must match the dict shape core.scorer.score_markets already consumes.
items.notes: Matching the existing input shape is the hard requirement. If reconstruction emits a different shape the scorer gets modified to accommodate it, which breaks comparability with live signals and defeats the point.

items.title: Retroactive Replay Runner
items.action: Drive backtesting/harness.py over the reconstructed corpus and grade each replayed score against the known settled outcome. Write results to a table physically separate from live paper signals.
items.notes: Replayed scores are contaminated by look-ahead: the model's training data and web search both know how these resolved. Separation must be enforced in schema, not by convention, so no later query can accidentally pool the two populations.

items.title: Instrument Validation On Replay Corpus
items.action: Using the replay corpus at n>=300, verify the measurement apparatus: grading handles early closes, voided markets and multi-outcome events; baseline Brier computes correctly across the full price range; the resolved_count >= 10 smart-money wallet threshold is empirically defensible.
items.notes: This tests the instrument, not the forecaster. Contaminated data cannot establish edge and no edge claim may cite it. The value is discovering broken grading logic now instead of at n=25 with live signals that took three months to accumulate.

items.title: Price-Blind Scoring Arm
items.action: Add a scoring mode that omits the Current market price line and all market-anchoring instructions. Run it on a sampled subset of scored markets daily and log to a separate column. Do not feed it into signal selection.
items.notes: Shadow arm only. Provides the counterfactual for whether the anchored scorer adds information over the price itself. Sample rate must stay inside the daily cost ceiling.

items.title: Unattended Operation Hardening
items.action: Alert on absence rather than presence: notify if no successful run has completed within N hours. Add graceful degradation when an upstream API changes shape, and a runbook in docs/ for diagnosing a failed run without reloading full project context.
items.notes: Target completion early October. Available maintenance time drops sharply after 2026-10-21 and the pipeline must survive unattended or data accumulation silently stops. Current failure mode is a scheduler that stops firing with nothing watching.

items.title: Public Methodology Writeup
items.action: Write a public methodology document covering the pipeline architecture, the market-price anchoring problem, the baseline comparison, and the pre-registered kill criteria. Publish for outside review.
items.notes: The price-anchoring issue survived months of solo review. Outside readers catch this class of problem quickly. Also converts the project from a private repo into something legible to a reader.

items.title: Multi-Sample Score Aggregation
items.action: Score each qualifying market N times independently and aggregate to a single estimate, replacing the single-pass call in score_markets.
items.notes: Single-pass scoring absorbs full LLM sampling variance into signal selection. Gated because it shifts the estimate distribution and makes pre-change and post-change resolutions non-comparable.

items.title: Power BI Export Schema Hardening
items.action: Add run_id to data/powerbi_export/signals.csv as a foreign key to runs.csv. Confirm the source column reliably distinguishes live paper signals from any future replay corpus rows. Audit every column where blank currently appears and document, per column, whether blank means not-computed or zero.
items.notes: signals.csv and runs.csv share no join key today, so Power BI cannot relate signals to the cost, token and runtime data in runs.csv, and joining on date is ambiguous because the pipeline runs twice daily. The source discriminator must exist and be filtered on before replay-runner produces look-ahead-contaminated rows; adding it while it has a single value is free, retrofitting it later means auditing every visual for silent contamination. Blank vs zero matters because Power BI AVERAGE skips blanks: net_edge and base_rate are blank on 37 of 182 rows, close_time on 25.
